topics.title: Canada faces holiday weather extremes
topics.summary: Canada is heading into Canada Day with flooding risks in the West, heavy rain in Saskatchewan and Manitoba, wildfire smoke near Calgary, and heat warnings in parts of Ontario and Quebec.
topics.discussionPrompt: Canada Day weather can be unpredictable. Would you rather plan around heat, smoke, rain, or just move the whole party indoors?

topics.title: Heat dome hits World Cup cities
topics.summary: A heat dome is bringing dangerous temperatures to World Cup host cities including Toronto, Kansas City, Philadelphia and East Rutherford, with hydration breaks and cooling plans in place.
topics.discussionPrompt: Big sports events are usually planned years ahead. Should extreme weather now be treated like a normal part of event planning?

topics.title: Morocco sets up Canada World Cup clash
topics.summary: Morocco beat the Netherlands 3-2 on penalties after a 1-1 draw, moving into a World Cup last-16 match against co-host Canada in Houston.
topics.discussionPrompt: Penalty shootouts are brutal and exciting. Do they feel like a fair test, or more like flipping a coin with cleats on?

topics.title: Oil forecasts ease after Hormuz reopening
topics.summary: A Reuters poll found analysts lowering 2026 oil price forecasts after the Strait of Hormuz reopened, easing supply concerns from earlier disruptions.
topics.discussionPrompt: It is strange how one narrow shipping route can affect prices everywhere. Do energy markets feel too fragile, or just naturally connected?
